## FHIR Queries — Glaucoma Patient Identification (Two-Resource Union)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|23986001,http://snomed.info/sct|84494001,http://snomed.info/sct|95725002
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600
```

---

### Query Breakdown

#### Query 1 — Condition (Glaucoma Diagnoses)

| SNOMED CT Code | Concept | Role in Query |
|---|---|---|
| `23986001` | Glaucoma (disorder) — broad concept | Catches any glaucoma subtype not explicitly listed |
| `84494001` | Open-angle glaucoma | Most prevalent form (~90% of glaucoma cases) |
| `95725002` | Primary angle-closure glaucoma | Pathophysiologically distinct; high IOP from angle obstruction |

Multiple codes are provided as a **comma-separated OR list** within the single `code` parameter — this is a single FHIR request returning all three variants in one bundle.

#### Query 2 — MedicationRequest (Timolol)

| RxNorm Code | Concept | Caveat |
|---|---|---|
| `10600` | Timolol (ingredient-level code) | Matches all formulations (ophthalmic drops, oral tablets) |

> ⚠️ **Known contamination risk:** Timolol is also prescribed for systemic hypertension and migraine prophylaxis. The medication cohort will yield **false positives** for the glaucoma phenotype. These should be resolved during the union step (e.g., patients appearing in both queries are high-confidence; medication-only patients require additional validation such as checking for ophthalmic route or co-occurring diagnoses).

---

### Post-Query Union Logic (Client-Side)

```
patient_set_A = { resource.subject.reference for resource in Condition results }
patient_set_B = { resource.subject.reference for resource in MedicationRequest results }
glaucoma_candidates = patient_set_A ∪ patient_set_B   # deduplicated union
high_confidence     = patient_set_A ∩ patient_set_B   # both diagnosis + medication
```
